ABCD_SubMod_ClustComp

Felicia Hardi

2025-07-24

Load packages

library(broom)
library(cluster)
library(corrplot)
library(dplyr)
library(factoextra)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(grid)
library(Hmisc)
library(kableExtra)
library(knitr)
library(magrittr)
library(NbClust)
library(psych)
library(purrr)
library(table1)
library(tidyverse)
library(viridis)

Hierarchical clustering

Estimate and compare clusters

# env_master: just environmental vars
psych::describe(env_master)
##                 vars     n    mean      sd  median trimmed     mad  min
## src_subject_id*    1 11772 5886.50 3398.43 5886.50 5886.50 4363.29 1.00
## lec_bad            2 11419    5.06    4.99    3.50    4.28    3.71 0.00
## dm                 3 11354    1.17    0.32    1.00    1.09    0.00 1.00
## ksads_ptsd         4 11745    0.72    1.07    0.00    0.54    0.00 0.00
## crpbi              5 11767    2.76    0.24    2.80    2.79    0.22 1.00
## pm                 6 11769    4.45    0.38    4.53    4.49    0.40 2.00
## abcl               7  9204   23.88   22.77   17.00   20.05   16.31 0.00
## asr                8 11771   20.90   16.60   16.50   18.54   13.34 0.00
## fes_conflict       9 11772    2.22    1.28    2.00    2.12    1.24 0.00
## fes_cohesion      10 10884    7.29    1.62    8.00    7.52    1.48 0.00
## fes_express       11 10884    6.02    1.45    6.00    6.11    1.48 0.00
## fes_int_cult      12 10884    6.12    1.95    6.00    6.23    1.48 0.00
## fes_org           13 10883    6.19    2.07    7.00    6.35    1.48 0.00
## familism          14 11772    3.71    0.51    3.72    3.72    0.50 1.00
## peers_prosocial   15 10867    3.18    0.90    3.33    3.18    0.99 1.00
## peers_rulebreak   16 10769    1.25    0.48    1.00    1.13    0.00 1.00
## pnh               17 10931   11.68    8.16   11.00   11.49   10.38 0.00
## srpf              18 11769    9.79    1.10    9.92    9.85    1.11 4.25
## peq               19 10942    4.12    1.40    3.67    3.86    0.99 3.00
## comc              20 10857    3.82    0.67    3.90    3.86    0.74 1.00
## parks             21 11218    0.05    0.09    0.02    0.03    0.02 0.00
## comm_res          22 11220    4.30    6.10    3.72    3.92    1.56 0.94
## nsc               23 11772    3.98    0.70    4.11    4.04    0.66 1.08
## nbhsoc            24 11220    0.11    0.11    0.08    0.09    0.06 0.01
## urban             25 11181    1.21    0.59    1.00    1.03    0.00 0.50
## lead              26 11209    5.13    3.16    5.00    5.02    4.45 1.00
## pm25              27 11210    7.71    1.70    7.75    7.66    1.51 1.72
## adi               28 10986   95.15   22.69   98.86   97.86   15.18 1.07
## coi_ed            29 10771   60.54   30.16   67.00   62.44   32.62 1.00
## coi_he            30 10771   58.64   30.48   63.00   60.14   37.06 1.00
## coi_se            31 10771   59.47   31.22   66.00   61.14   37.06 1.00
##                      max    range  skew kurtosis    se
## src_subject_id* 11772.00 11771.00  0.00    -1.20 31.32
## lec_bad            47.50    47.50  1.98     6.11  0.05
## dm                  4.43     3.43  3.28    14.26  0.00
## ksads_ptsd         17.00    17.00  4.66    53.23  0.01
## crpbi               3.00     2.00 -1.38     2.40  0.00
## pm                  5.00     3.00 -1.06     1.52  0.00
## abcl              195.00   195.00  1.85     4.44  0.24
## asr               154.00   154.00  1.55     3.33  0.15
## fes_conflict        8.00     8.00  0.71     0.32  0.01
## fes_cohesion        9.00     9.00 -1.37     2.29  0.02
## fes_express         9.00     9.00 -0.60     0.14  0.01
## fes_int_cult        9.00     9.00 -0.53    -0.24  0.02
## fes_org             9.00     9.00 -0.63    -0.28  0.02
## familism            5.00     4.00 -0.21     0.20  0.00
## peers_prosocial     5.00     4.00 -0.09    -0.53  0.01
## peers_rulebreak     5.00     4.00  3.09    12.75  0.00
## pnh                27.00    27.00  0.08    -1.14  0.08
## srpf               12.00     7.75 -0.66     0.55  0.01
## peq                15.00    12.00  2.23     7.30  0.01
## comc                5.00     4.00 -0.57     0.28  0.01
## parks               0.92     0.92  3.51    16.07  0.00
## comm_res          601.47   600.53 83.62  8161.94  0.06
## nsc                 5.00     3.92 -0.87     0.55  0.01
## nbhsoc              0.80     0.80  2.03     4.68  0.00
## urban               6.00     5.50  2.68     6.34  0.01
## lead               20.00    19.00  0.33    -0.78  0.03
## pm25               23.77    22.04  1.03     6.34  0.02
## adi               247.43   246.36 -0.84     6.35  0.22
## coi_ed            198.00   197.00 -0.39    -0.67  0.29
## coi_he            200.00   199.00 -0.28    -0.86  0.29
## coi_se            196.00   195.00 -0.30    -0.85  0.30
# create df w vars of interest only and standardize
env_master_clust = data.frame(scale(env_master[2:31], center=F))

# examine dendogram
dist_matrix = dist(na.omit(env_master_clust), method = "manhattan")
hc = hclust(dist_matrix, method = "ward.D2")
plot(hc, hang = -1, label=F, xlab="Data points")

# compare cluster indices
hc_nbclust_env = NbClust(data = env_master_clust,
                         distance = "manhattan", min.nc = 2, max.nc = 5, method = "ward.D2", index = c("all")) # k=3

## *** : The Hubert index is a graphical method of determining the number of clusters.
##                 In the plot of Hubert index, we seek a significant knee that corresponds to a 
##                 significant increase of the value of the measure i.e the significant peak in Hubert
##                 index second differences plot. 
## 

## *** : The D index is a graphical method of determining the number of clusters. 
##                 In the plot of D index, we seek a significant knee (the significant peak in Dindex
##                 second differences plot) that corresponds to a significant increase of the value of
##                 the measure. 
##  
## ******************************************************************* 
## * Among all indices:                                                
## * 5 proposed 2 as the best number of clusters 
## * 6 proposed 3 as the best number of clusters 
## * 5 proposed 4 as the best number of clusters 
## * 4 proposed 5 as the best number of clusters 
## 
##                    ***** Conclusion *****                            
##  
## * According to the majority rule, the best number of clusters is  3 
##  
##  
## *******************************************************************
hc_nbclust_env
## $All.index
##       KL       CH Hartigan     CCC    Scott      Marriot  TrCovW   TraceW
## 2 0.8516 771.7379 695.9768 93.0811 57627.46 2.470847e+83 4728299 39499.75
## 3 0.8556 767.0160 746.1913 64.8168 62649.30 2.985665e+83 3582368 36365.81
## 4 2.8049 807.2265 335.8506 69.1761 68410.46 2.601259e+83 2653884 33289.59
## 5 3.1052 714.4774 168.3039 72.9273 79298.41 1.055938e+83 2541125 31960.16
##   Friedman  Rubin Cindex     DB Silhouette   Duda Pseudot2  Beale Ratkowsky
## 2 1039.517 5.8679 0.2445 2.5169     0.1838 0.9021 717.1871 2.2952    0.1466
## 3 1042.607 6.3736 0.2383 2.4969     0.1392 0.8534 915.0964 3.6316    0.1791
## 4 1044.494 6.9625 0.2308 2.2596     0.0995 0.9101 457.8241 2.0876    0.1667
## 5 1059.650 7.2521 0.2227 2.3051     0.0937 0.9172 115.2742 1.9072    0.1610
##        Ball Ptbiserial    Frey McClain   Dunn Hubert SDindex Dindex   SDbw
## 2 19749.877     0.3508  0.6040  0.3412 0.0740      0  2.3308 2.0573 0.9771
## 3 12121.935     0.4232  0.8985  0.7988 0.0737      0  2.2761 1.9822 1.1015
## 4  8322.399     0.4182  0.8066  1.1799 0.0726      0  2.3525 1.9106 1.0832
## 5  6392.032     0.3997 -0.1613  1.7933 0.0715      0  2.4326 1.8656 0.9774
## 
## $All.CriticalValues
##   CritValue_Duda CritValue_PseudoT2 Fvalue_Beale
## 2         0.9688           213.0723        1e-04
## 3         0.9676           178.2889        0e+00
## 4         0.9668           159.1403        4e-04
## 5         0.9560            58.7873        2e-03

## [8030] 3 3 1 3 3 1 3 1 2 3 3 3 2 2 1 3 1 2 2 1 1 1 1 1 1 3 2 1 3 2 3 2 1 1 3 1 3
## [8067] 3 3 1 3 1 2 1 3 3 1 1 1
# cut tree to best number of solution
clust_assignments = cutree(hc, k = 3)
table(clust_assignments)
## clust_assignments
##    1    2    3 
## 5329 1279 1470
plot(hc, hang = -1, label=F, xlab="Data points")
rect.hclust(hc, k = 3, border = c("#7C5B54", "#F3D8C7", "grey"))

Extract clusters

agnes_res_clust = cbind(data.frame(src_subject_id = na.omit(env_master)$src_subject_id, clust = hc_nbclust_env$Best.partition))

Join with main data frame

agnes_res_env = left_join(agnes_res_clust, env_master_full_wbrain)
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id)`
agnes_res_env %>% count(clust)
##   clust    n
## 1     1 5329
## 2     2 1279
## 3     3 1470
# label the vars
# create table for prevalence rate
label(agnes_res_env$lec_bad) = "Negative life events"
label(agnes_res_env$dm) = "Discrimination"
label(agnes_res_env$ksads_ptsd) = "Traumatic events"
label(agnes_res_env$crpbi) = "Parenting"
label(agnes_res_env$pm) = "Parental monitoring"
label(agnes_res_env$asr) = "Parent psychopathology"
label(agnes_res_env$abcl) = "Other parent psychopathology"
label(agnes_res_env$fes_conflict) = "Family conflict"
label(agnes_res_env$fes_cohesion) = "Family cohesion"
label(agnes_res_env$fes_express) = "Family expressiveness"
label(agnes_res_env$fes_int_cult) = "Family int/cultural engagement"
label(agnes_res_env$fes_org) = "Family organization"
label(agnes_res_env$peers_prosocial) = "Prosocial peers"
label(agnes_res_env$peers_rulebreak) = "Rule-breaking peers"
label(agnes_res_env$pnh) = "Protective peer network"
label(agnes_res_env$srpf) = "Protective school environment"
label(agnes_res_env$peq) = "Peer aggression experience"
label(agnes_res_env$familism) = "Familism"
label(agnes_res_env$comc) = "Collective efficacy"
label(agnes_res_env$nsc) = "Neighborhood safety"
label(agnes_res_env$comm_res) = "Community resources"
label(agnes_res_env$parks) = "Parks"
label(agnes_res_env$nbhsoc) = "Neighborhood disadvantage"
label(agnes_res_env$urban) = "Urbanicity"
label(agnes_res_env$lead) = "Lead risk"
label(agnes_res_env$pm25) = "Air pollution (PM 2.5)"
label(agnes_res_env$adi) = "Area deprivation index"
label(agnes_res_env$coi_ed) = "Child opportunity index (education)"
label(agnes_res_env$coi_he) = "Child opportunity index (health)"
label(agnes_res_env$coi_se) = "Child opportunity index (social/economic)"

Compare environmental factors across clusters/groups

Table and correlation plot

agnes_res_env_tab = table1(~ lec_bad + dm + ksads_ptsd + 
                             crpbi + pm + asr + abcl + 
                             fes_conflict + fes_cohesion + fes_express + fes_int_cult + fes_org + familism + 
                             peers_prosocial + peers_rulebreak + pnh + srpf + peq + 
                             comc + parks + comm_res + nsc + nbhsoc + 
                             urban + lead + pm25 + adi + coi_ed + coi_he + coi_se | as.factor(clust), 
                           data=agnes_res_env)
agnes_res_env_tab
1
(N=5329)		 2
(N=1279)		 3
(N=1470)		 Overall

## correlation plot
# reorder variables
agnes_res_env[3:32] = agnes_res_env[c("lec_bad", "dm", "ksads_ptsd", 
                                      "crpbi", "pm", "asr", "abcl", 
                                      "fes_conflict", "fes_cohesion", "fes_express", "fes_int_cult", "fes_org", "familism",
                                      "peers_prosocial", "peers_rulebreak", "pnh", "srpf", "peq",
                                      "comc", "parks", "comm_res", "nsc", "nbhsoc", 
                                      "urban", "lead", "pm25", "adi", "coi_ed", "coi_he", "coi_se")]

varnames = c(
  "Negative life events", "Discrimination", "Traumatic events", 
  "Parenting", "Parental monitoring", "Parent psychopathology", "Other parent psychopathology",
  "Family conflict", "Family cohesion", "Family expressiveness", "Family int/cultural engagement", "Family organization", "Familism", 
  "Prosocial peers", "Rule-breaking peers", "Protective peer network", "Protective school environment", "Peer experience", 
  "Collective efficacy", "Parks", "Community resources", "Neighborhood safety", "Neighborhood disadvantage", 
  "Urbanicity", "Lead risk", "Air pollution (PM 2.5)", "Area deprivation index",
  "Child opportunity index (education)", "Child opportunity index (health)", "Child opportunity index (social/economic)")

colnames(agnes_res_env)[3:32] = varnames
cor_matrix = cor(agnes_res_env[3:32])
corrplot(cor_matrix, 
  method = "color", addCoef.col = "black", type="upper", 
  number.cex = 0.5, number.font = 1, tl.col="black")

Visualize prevalence plot for each profile

# create subsets for each row of the figure
fig1_long_2 = agnes_res_env %>%
  mutate(across(3:32, ~ scale(.x, center=F))) %>%
  select(c(1:32)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = 3:32, names_to = "variable", values_to = "value")
fig1_long_2$variable = factor(fig1_long_2$variable, levels = varnames)
fig1_long_2$clust = as.factor(fig1_long_2$clust)

subset_1 = varnames[1:3]
subset_2 = varnames[4:7]
subset_3 = varnames[8:13]
subset_4 = varnames[14:18]
subset_5 = varnames[19:23]
subset_6 = varnames[24:30]

viridis_col = viridis(6) 
custom_colors = c("1" = "grey", "2" = "#F3D8C7", "3" = "#7C5B54") 

# create plots for each row
plot_row_1 =
  fig1_long_2 %>% filter(variable %in% subset_1) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = variable, y = value, fill = clust, group = clust)) +
  annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = viridis_col[6], alpha = 0.75) +
  stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "bar", position = "dodge", alpha = 0.7, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2, width = 0.8) + 
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_boot", geom = "errorbar", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.25) +
  theme_minimal() + labs(title = "", x = "Variable", y = "Standardized Value") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) + 
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"))
plot_row_2 =
  fig1_long_2 %>% filter(variable %in% subset_2) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = variable, y = value, fill = clust, group = clust)) +
  annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = viridis_col[5], alpha = 0.75) +
  stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "bar", position = "dodge", alpha = 0.7, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2, width = 0.8) + 
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_boot", geom = "errorbar", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.25) +
  theme_minimal() + labs(title = "", x = "Variable", y = "Standardized Value") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) + 
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"))
plot_row_3 = 
  fig1_long_2 %>% filter(variable %in% subset_3) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = variable, y = value, fill = clust, group = clust)) +
  annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = viridis_col[4], alpha = 0.75) +
  stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "bar", position = "dodge", alpha = 0.7, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2, width = 0.8) + 
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_boot", geom = "errorbar", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.25) +
  theme_minimal() + labs(title = "", x = "Variable", y = "Standardized Value") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) + 
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"))
plot_row_4 = 
  fig1_long_2 %>% filter(variable %in% subset_4) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = variable, y = value, fill = clust, group = clust)) +
  annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = viridis_col[3], alpha = 0.75) +
  stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "bar", position = "dodge", alpha = 0.7, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2, width = 0.8) + 
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_boot", geom = "errorbar", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.25) +
  theme_minimal() + labs(title = "", x = "Variable", y = "Standardized Value") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) + 
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"))
plot_row_5 = 
  fig1_long_2 %>% filter(variable %in% subset_5) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = variable, y = value, fill = clust, group = clust)) +
  annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = viridis_col[2], alpha = 0.75) +
  stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "bar", position = "dodge", alpha = 0.7, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2, width = 0.8) + 
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_boot", geom = "errorbar", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.25) +
  theme_minimal() + labs(title = "", x = "Variable", y = "Standardized Value") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) + 
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"))
plot_row_6 = 
  fig1_long_2 %>% filter(variable %in% subset_6) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = variable, y = value, fill = clust, group = clust)) +
  annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = viridis_col[1], alpha = 0.5) +
  stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "bar", position = "dodge", alpha = 0.7, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2, width = 0.8) + 
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_boot", geom = "errorbar", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.25) +
  theme_minimal() + labs(title = "", x = "Variable", y = "Standardized Value") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) + 
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"))

# display altogether
plot_row_1

plot_row_2

plot_row_3

plot_row_4

plot_row_5

plot_row_6
